dashcam lane detection flickers to count nine lanes on two lane road

photojournalism.newsExtreme.title: News extreme
photojournalism.newsExtreme.description: Catastrophic, world-altering, and extreme global news events and disasters
examples: a massive synthetic biology lab leak creates a highly aggressive, invasive plant species that destroys concrete
a major celebrity's private life is exposed in a major scandal
a major dam collapses, unleashing a catastrophic wall of water onto downstream towns
a major new discovery in the world of quantum physics
a major new discovery in the world of astrophysics
an experimental commercial spacecraft explodes on its maiden passenger voyage to orbit

photojournalism.newsNormal.title: News normal
photojournalism.newsNormal.description: Standard major news headlines covering science, politics, business, and society
examples: a high-profile court trial of a corrupt politician begins, attracting massive media crowds
a breakthrough in the fight against cancer or other major diseases
a major scientific conference, featuring presentations by top researchers from around the world
a historic election, with record voter turnout and a groundbreaking candidate
a major financial crisis, such as a stock market crash or a bank bailout
a major global health crisis, such as the spread of a new virus or the resurgence of an old one

photojournalism.newsSpecific.title: News specific
photojournalism.newsSpecific.description: Highly detailed, specific descriptions of major global news events and crises
examples: a critical space rescue: an international crew of astronauts executes a daring spacewalk to repair a damaged life-support system
a major natural disaster: a powerful hurricane slams into the coast of florida, causing massive flooding and leaving hundreds of thousands of residents without power
a major high-tech art heist: armed thieves bypass advanced security systems to steal a famous modern masterpiece from a gallery in paris
a major celebrity scandal: a top hollywood actor is caught in a massive cheating scandal, leading to the collapse of his marriage and a major hit to his career
a landmark supreme court ruling: justices hand down a historic decision on privacy rights in the digital age, limiting government surveillance
a political protest: demonstrators gather outside the white house to protest the government's immigration policies, calling for greater protection for undocumented immigrants and refugees

photojournalism.styleProtest.title: Style protest
photojournalism.styleProtest.description: Various photographic styles and techniques used in photojournalism and documentary photography
examples: unposed candid portraiture: capturing subjects unaware of the camera to document authentic, unvarnished human reactions and interactions
monochrome photojournalism: stripping away color to emphasize form, texture, shadow, and the pure raw emotion of the moment
documentary photography: providing a historical record of the protests and the people involved
analog film photography: using traditional 35mm or medium format film to create a raw, grain-textured aesthetic with natural color tones
high-speed photography: using fast shutter speeds to capture fast-paced action and movement
close-up portraiture: focusing tightly on the expressive faces of individual protesters to highlight intense human emotion and determination

photojournalism.types.title: Types of photojournalism
photojournalism.types.description: Diverse subjects and thematic beats covered in photojournalism and documentary reporting
examples: community and neighborhood storytelling, highlighting the everyday lives and challenges of local residents
climate migration and climate refugees, documenting families forced to relocate due to environmental changes
photojournalistic documentation of social movements and protests, including black lives matter and the women's march

set.moviegenBench.title: Benchmark prompt set from MovieGenBench
set.moviegenBench.description: MovieGen Video Bench contains 1003 diverse prompts testing various aspects including human activities (motion, emotions), animals, nature/scenery, physics (fluids, gravity, collisions), and unusual subjects/activities. The prompts provide comprehensive coverage across high, medium and low motion scenarios.
examples: A deer in a fancy ballroom dress, waltzing with a fox under a chandelier.
Cinematic trailer for a group of adventurous puppies exploring ruins in the sky

This close-up shot of a Victoria crowned pigeon showcases its striking blue plumage and red chest. Its crest is made of delicate, lacy feathers, while its eye is a striking red color. The bird’s head is tilted slightly to the side, giving the impression of it looking regal and majestic. The background is blurred, drawing attention to the bird’s striking appearance.
The person's cheeks flushed with pleasure as she savored a delicious meal.
A person is brushing their teeth in front of a mirror, their mouth slightly open as they clean each tooth.
A fencer engaged in a fast-paced duel.

the statue of Liberty next to the Washington Monument
a red swirl above a black dashed line.
a spaceship landing on the Great Wall

thing.cursedObject.title: Cursed objects

sock that is always inside out after wash
garden gnome that faces the window each morning
cracked tamagotchi that asks if you are alone
